response: To cut out sugar from your daily diet, start by reading food labels and avoiding [sweetened beverages] and [processed snacks]. Replace sugary treats with fruits or nuts. Check for hidden sugars in sauces and dressings. Use natural sweeteners like honey or maple syrup in moderation. Gradually reduce sugar intake to avoid cravings. Drink plenty of water to curb sweet cravings. Choose whole, unprocessed foods whenever possible. [Meal replacements] can help manage hunger without added sugar.
